subject: Computer Networking
title: AI-Driven Traffic Engineering
description: A short lesson to help you apply AI-Driven Traffic Engineering.

hook.text: Traditional routing picks the shortest path. AI considers congestion, latency, jitter, and demand.

buildup.text: AI models analyze traffic across the network in real time. They predict congestion before it hits.

discovery.text: Instead of reacting to a full link, AI reroutes preemptively. Users never feel the avoided jam.

twist.text: Google uses ML to optimize global backbone traffic. Millions saved by maximizing link utilization.

climax.text: Reinforcement learning experiments: try a route, measure results, improve. The network learns.

punchline.text: AI routes traffic before problems appear. Proactive, not reactive.

quiz.question: How does AI-driven traffic engineering differ from traditional routing?
options: It predicts and prevents congestion before it happens
It always chooses the shortest physical path
It routes all traffic through a single central server
